title: taraxacum pseudo-officinale
intro: Taraxacum pseudo-officinale, also known as the lesser dandelion, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate the liver and gallbladder, support digestion, and act as a diuretic. The lesser dandelion has been used to treat a range of health issues, including liver and gallbladder problems, skin conditions, and urinary tract infections, due to its high content of vitamins A, C, and D, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice among herbalists and natural health practitioners.
